column1: New track coming out shortly. Don't expect any changes. This will be an "East-weighted" system with heavy rains setting up West of I-75. We'll post the update when it's released.
Thanks for keeping us up to date

Update: we are about 3 hours from Disney on 95 south about to enter Florida. The worst weather was last evening driving in very heavy rain and traffic in Maryland and Virginia. Grateful to get here ahead of the storm. It might be a rainy week in Disney, but we’re OK with that! Thank you so much to everyone who gave their suggestions. Truly appreciate your advice and thoughts.
Will I be ok driving south on I-95 from Savannah to Orlando tomorrow afternoon. I can’t read any times on the maps for when the storm hits that area.
tomorrow is supposed to be heavier day for rain in Florida....just saying

As always, THE best and ONLY source for weather in the Tampa area. #rule#7
Sitting high on the Bluffs over the Intercoastal where we always get the high end of the winds. Clearing the yard of potted plants. What few I've kept over the years.

The center of circulation is well east of where they’re indicating it to be.
Makes me wonder if it’ll track east of the forecast.
it's definitely going to get stronger once on the other side of the islands and warmer water, turn east a bit. Then they call it a hurricane. My opinion.
That's what I'm thinking too - Not too far east, but enough to feel some of the effects of the storm

and if it’s stronger than they’re expecting, wouldn’t that make a harder right turn as well?
Idk. Just thinking this thing hasn’t been easy to predict so far.
Who knows?

Dennis we are in the siesta key area needing to head back to Tampa over the Sunshine skyway bridge tomorrow. Was wondering what the winds are projected to be in the morning concerned about them closing the bridge.

Siesta Key has areas that ALWAYs flood, so you may have trouble even getting off the island tomorrow. All of the East/West main roads in Sarasota and Bradenton also have problem areas that flood in our normal summer storms, so be sure to plan some alternate routes to get to the Interstate and add in some extra time. Don’t even think about going over the skyway tomorrow-it’s pretty awful with heavy winds and rain, long before it gets officially closed- which is usually around 30mph.
may have to take the long way around. They close the bridge at 35 mph winds I think
thanks yeah you live in Trinity so that would definitely be the long way but it is an option.
I would leave today for a cornucopia of reasons.
just take 75 around
you can go back on 75 through Riverview if they close the bridge.
leave now

Crystal River rock crusher canyon Rd ok? Literally just bought a RV travel trailer that I live in

I've stayed put during Tropical storms and a Cat 1 in my OLD leaking travel trailer and I was ok. I've been more terrified during a really bad afternoon thunderstorm. The tropical storm was direct and the cat 1 was either to the north or south a ways. Your best bet if you're in an RV park is to talk to your neighbors. They'll tell you what to do. Mine did.
Keep it more west as it goes up the coast like Idalia did.
